1.description: Posterior variances of unknown parameters in(κ0t,κPt)from the Bayesian learning rule in GLS. The dashed lines correspond to the learning rule whereBEtreatsΘ0as constant, and the solid lines to the case in whichBEbelieves thatΘtfollows a random walk. [Color figure can be viewed atwileyonlinelibrary.com]

2.description: Decomposition of the “long-run means” of the 10-year yieldyt10implied by ruleℓCGL(P). The mean ofPtin monthtis set to(I−κPt)−1κ0tand contribution of eachPCjis its loadingBj10ymultiplied by its pseudo long-run mean. [Color figure can be viewed atwileyonlinelibrary.com]

3.description: Interdecile disagreement about one-year-ahead two- and 10-year bond yields for “All,” “Financial,” and “Consulting” professionals in the BCFF survey. [Color figure can be viewed atwileyonlinelibrary.com]

4.description: Fraction of respondents in the bottom 20% who transition to the top 20%, middle 60%, or bottom 20% ranges one year hence. The upper two graphs correspond to yield forecasts and the lower two graphs to absolute forecast errors. [Color figure can be viewed atwileyonlinelibrary.com]

5.description: Frequency distributions of the estimates ofβFR1m9for all BCFF forecasters. The solid vertical line corresponds to the medianβFR1m9, while the dashed line to the estimate for the BayesianBE. [Color figure can be viewed atwileyonlinelibrary.com]

6.description: Frequency distributions of the estimates ofβFR2m9for all BCFF forecasters. The solid vertical line corresponds to the medianβFR2m9, while the dashed line to the estimate for the BayesianBE. [Color figure can be viewed atwileyonlinelibrary.com]

7.description: Fitted projections ofBE's forecast errors. Panels A to F display the fitted projections ofBE's forecast errors ontoIt=PtorIt=(Pt,Ht,FRtBE(f))for the one-year (left) and 10-year (right) bond yields. [Color figure can be viewed atwileyonlinelibrary.com]

8.description: Fitted projections ofCP's forecast errors. This figure compares fitted projections ofCP's forecast errors ofEHm(left panels) andTPm(right panels) ontoPt,(Pt,Ht), or(Pt,Ht,FRt(f))one-year bond yields. [Color figure can be viewed atwileyonlinelibrary.com]

9.description: This figure examines relationships between yield and macro disagreements. First row: fraction of BCFF professions holding the 20% highest one-year-ahead yield forecasts (form=10 years) who fall into the top 20%, mid 60%, and bottom 20% of forecasts for real GDP growth and inflation. Second and third rows: comparing the macro disagreements of those BCFF forecasters who determine the yield disagreementsID(PC1)(left panels) orID(PC2)(right panels) with unrestricted macro disagreements. [Color figure can be viewed atwileyonlinelibrary.com]

10.description: Term structures of disagreement. This figure shows the term structures of disagreement about the 10-year Treasury bond yield and the growth rate of real GDP. Sample: January 1984 to December 2018. [Color figure can be viewed atwileyonlinelibrary.com]

11.description: Realized forecast errors fromCP. This figures showsCP's realized forecast errors for theFFR(Panel A) and one-year yield (Panel B) over a nine-month horizon. [Color figure can be viewed atwileyonlinelibrary.com]

12.description: Expected excess returns on the 10-year bond. This figure shows the time series of expected excess returns on the 10-year bond over a one-year horizon forBE(GPS's ruleℓCG(P)),CP, and the forecaster who has the median reaction to revisions in expectations aboutPC1nine months ahead (FR(PC1;9,3)). [Color figure can be viewed atwileyonlinelibrary.com]

13.description: Frequency distributions of mean forecast errors andα̂m,9. Form=2yand10y, this figure shows the frequency distributions of mean forecast errors of the BCFF forecasters over a nine-month horizon (left panel) and fitted intercepts in the time-series projections of individual forecast errors onto a constant andFR(f;9,3)(right panel). [Color figure can be viewed atwileyonlinelibrary.com]

14.description: Frequency distributions of fittedβfm,9. This figures shows the frequency distributions of fittedβfm,9forPC1andPC2from projections of individual forecast errors over a nine-month horizon onto(1,Pt′)for them=2yand10ybond yields. [Color figure can be viewed atwileyonlinelibrary.com]
